reviews.review_description:
I traveled without controller so forced find the hand tracking game. This is amazing. 

The thumbnail pic is kinda misleading. It’s 3D puzzle game. And it is so well designed both mechanically and visually.

And fun, challenging but not frustrating. 

Dunno why this doesn’t come up and recommended more often. So much better than cubism.

I had great time with this game. Nice stylish graphics. Good controls, you can freely rotate playing area. Creative ideas for puzzle, to get 3-star on some level you really need to think outside of the box. Highly recommended.
My favorite genre of game is Puzzlers and this one is supper clever. Each puzzle is 100% different from the last, keeping it very fresh. Give it a go. Have a feeling most people will fall in love.
Very cool game that makes you think and reflect.

I have spent about 7 hours playing through all 80 stages.

See my video review here: https://youtu.be/bPWJyfLP0s8

Or search ‘VR Reviews PathCraft Quest 2 Review’

Don’t let the child-themed art style fool you, this game can get quite challenging!

Guide a young boy across platforms and complete each level by collecting all of the power cells within. 

That sounds simple enough except you don’t have direct control over the child - instead he will walk mindlessly in a straight line and you have to direct him by creating a tailored path using strategically placed blocks.

There’s a level editor to create puzzles of your own or to play other people’s creations. You can also earn coins by completing puzzles in as few moves as possible, then redeem them for extra costumes for your character.

PathCraft delivers carefully designed and thought-provoking puzzle-platforming action that’s good for players of all ages.

Very good art design choice, It's cute. Back to childhood for me !
But It's not an easy game ! the challenge is here. Good progression level.

May be my best discovery from the store.
Recommended for young and old :)

Do not be fooled by it's cuteness,
it gets very challenging!
Brilliant!

Fun and cute game that reminds me a little bit of Lemmings. The puzzles are fun and challenging but never overwhelming (though I haven't finished all puzzles yet). I love the hand-painted style and the level of details in the environments- You don't want to leave the game once you're in it.

reviews.review_title: Pretty puzzle with a lot of variety
reviews.review_description:
**Will update the review once I'm deeper in the game**

The game is very pretty, smooth and the music is charming. Everything fits together very well, like a puzzle!

Talking about puzzles, the game itself present puzzle with increasing difficulty. The complexity of the puzzles varies from a very simple straight puzzle, to multiple turns and even horizontal levels.

The mechanics are super simple making it a perfect game for the family or to play sitting on a week night where you just want to free your mind and have some pure fun.

Grab the game, it's a great fun!

Oh WOW.  This game sure brought back memories for me. When I was younger (a lot younger) I used to play this game called 'chips challenge" on the Atari lynx. Of course that game was in 2D.  This game has similar vibes to that as well as a game called "lemmings". So a big thank you to the developers for bringing back some pleasant nostalgic memories for me  THANK YOU 👍👍👍. 

How come there are no community made maps available yet? Also it would be really helpful if you could rotate the platform so that you can look at the paltan from different angles to solve. And finally: and if it is possible, it would be a bonus if there was a mixed reality mode.

This is a very fun challenging Puzzler. Moving blocks to get to places around the map to collect crystals. you can use your hands or controllers but as the speed ramps up controllers are best. Plenty of levels and unlockables to get through and it does get very difficult at times.

Comes with a level editor and you can also play other peoples levels they create so a never ending game. 

Gameplay: https://youtu.be/gcx_9POtx1Q

Ok so don’t think this game is just for children. Graphics are really good and very vibrant. The theme here is of the child’s imagination, where by everything is made from cardboard, come on we all loved big card board boxes as kids 😁

Put that all a side this game is very challenging, yes it starts of easy to ease you in but as you progress the challenge gets real. You have these 3D puzzles that you have to navigate the child through to the lanterns.

There is also an editor mode, this lets you create your own levels and share them with the PathCraft community for endless fun.

I don’t think anyone would have any issues with motion sickness either playing this.

I do have a video up on my YouTube channel called fun with the clicks if you were interested in some gameplay.

It’s harder than I thought, like others said “very challenging”.

The interaction is very straightforward, and you can understand the game logic immediately in first few levels.

The visual is very nice and comfortable, with nice background music.

I haven’t completed all levels yet, but I will keep trying in coming days, Wish me luck! And 100% recommend this puzzle game to others!

25% link: https://tiny.url/pathcraft

This is a fun game with slight shades of Lemmings: you have to guide a boy from start to finish by modifying the path he travels. He starts off blocked and once you unblock him he’s off; you had better build the path faster than he can walk! Early levels are generous with extra blocks giving you time to solve the puzzle before he is in motion, although you are trying to get the fastest time on the leaderboard. Good   Casual fun you can drop in to easily.
Great game to relax and think to. I only wished that it was possible to rotate the board to see different angles to plot your moves !

reviews.review_title: A Surprising and Engaging VR Experience
reviews.review_description: I recently played this VR game on my Oculus Quest 2 and I was impressed by the experience. The game is a true nugget and a pleasant surprise. The 3D kid-friendly environment is beautifully rendered and the puzzles are challenging but not impossible. The game offers a vista, a new horizon of possibilities and fun. I highly recommend it to anyone looking for a fun and engaging VR experience. 5 stars!

Not that far into it yet but I already know that I like it the reason that I didn't give it five stars is the same reason as many others has said you can't rotate the puzzle you're up down left right back and forth but you can't rotate it makes it very frustrating okay

I usually don't buy games ...only play the ones that come with metaquest plus subscription but this was definitely worth it my kids really enjoy it but so do I. Even though I bought it for them I play it a lot too.
25% discount bio. link/hengzty No space in link

I just bought PathCraft for my Oculus Quest 2 and I have to say, I am impressed! I was blown away by the level of quality and attention to detail in the game. The environments are absolutely beautiful and the high refresh rate and clear image make for an great immersive experience.
This is a great gateway for those new to VR. It is easy to pick up and won't make you dizzy.
The gameplay itself is a lot of fun, but don't be fooled - it gets challenging quickly. The level editor is a great addition and allows for endless possibilities of play. The cute character and lovely costumes are just the cherry on top. Overall, I highly recommend it to any VR enthusiast and newcomer.
